channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon, Ashley Ahn and Pranav Baskar

item.title: A Potent Threat in Strait of Hormuz: Iran’s “Mosquito Fleet”
item.description: Separate from the regular Iranian Navy, with boats that often go more than 115 miles per hour, it’s what a retired U.S. official calls a “disruptive force.”
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps speedboat and navy ship in the Persian Gulf, in 2024.

item.title: Trump Extends Sanctions Exemption on Some Russian Oil as High Gas Prices Persist
item.description: The Trump administration made the announcement hours after Iran said that the Strait of Hormuz was open to commercial ships.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Wednesday that the U.S. government would not renew the sanctions exemption on Russian oil already at sea.

item.title: Trump Frames Iran War as All but Over in Optimistic Social Media Flurry
item.description: Iranian officials did not confirm most of Mr. Trump’s claims and disputed several of them.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump during an event in Las Vegas on Thursday.

item.title: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is open, but adds that it is still under Tehran’s supervision.
item.description: Ab
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Attendees at a rally in support of Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader, in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: Amid Conflicting Messages From Trump and Iran, Hopes for Peace Deal Emerge
item.description: On a day when both Iran and the United States declared the Strait of Hormuz opened, hopes for an agreement rose. But statements from President Trump and Iranian leaders about negotiations were sometimes at odds.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi, Peter Eavis, Pranav Baskar and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally in support of Iran’s leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: The U.S. blockade on ships will continue until Trump ends it, says Central Command chief.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: Adm. Brad Cooper, the head of U.S. Central Command, during a news conference at the Pentagon on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Is Urged to Move on Nuclear Site Thought to Be Beyond Reach of Bombs
item.description: Little is known about Pickaxe Mountain, but some experts say it illustrates the impossibility of relying on force alone to prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear bomb.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: A satellite image provided by Vantor shows the tunnel complex at Pickaxe Mountain in Iran last month. Last fall, satellite images revealed that Iran had advanced construction at the site soon after U.S. forces disabled the country’s three main nuclear facilities in June.

item.title: Europe Wants to Help Restore Shipping in the Strait. It Just Isn’t Sure How.
item.description: Amid conflicting reports about the situation in the Strait of Hormuz, European leaders gathered on Friday to coordinate a plan to guard it.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: From left, Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany, President Emmanuel Macron of France, Prime Minister Keir Starmer of the United Kingdom and Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy arriving for a conference on navigation through the Strait of Hormuz at the Élysée Palace in Paris on Friday.

item.title: Why Lebanon’s Government Has Struggled to Contain Hezbollah
item.description: Lebanon’s government has been caught between Western demands to disarm the Shiite Muslim militant group and fears of inflaming sectarian tensions.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Hezbollah flag at the site of an Israeli airstrike in Beirut, Lebanon, last month.

item.title: Oil Prices Fall Sharply After Iran Says Strait of Hormuz Is Open
item.description: But analysts said it was not clear how quickly the oil industry in the Persian Gulf would be able to get back to normal.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: U.K. Police Investigate Claims Drones Targeted Israeli Embassy in London
item.description: The Metropolitan Police said that the building had not been attacked but that they were assessing “discarded items” in Kensington Gardens, a nearby park, which was cordoned off.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Officers in Kensington Gardens in London on Friday.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Is Open During Cease-Fire in Lebanon, U.S. and Iran Say
item.description: Statements from President Trump and Iran aimed to raise confidence in the safety of the waterway, but shipping experts said risks remained.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis, Jenny Gross and Joe Rennison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Oil tankers and cargo ships line up in the Strait of Hormuz as seen from the United Arab Emirates in March.

item.title: ‘I Just Want to Be Back’: Thousands Rush South in Lebanon Under Cease-Fire
item.description: Lebanese people who had been displaced by fighting expressed a mix of excitement and uncertainty about a pause in Israel’s campaign against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Hwaida Saad and David Guttenfelder
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Israel-Lebanon Cease-Fire: What to Know
item.description: The pause in fighting would remove a major hindrance to the U.S.-Iran peace talks, if it holds.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Families make their way home on Friday after the announcement of a cease-fire in Lebanon.

item.title: In northern Israel, relief at the cease-fire is overshadowed by unease.
item.description: Residents are wary that the truce may offer only a temporary reprieve after weeks of rocket fire by Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A street in the northern city of Kiryat Shmona, near Israel’s border with Lebanon, on Friday.

item.title: Lebanon’s Cease-Fire Buys Time, Not a Way Out
item.description: The country’s leadership secured a truce through risky talks with Israel, but the government now finds itself caught between competing pressures.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A family returning to Dahiya, Lebanon, after the cease-fire on Friday.

item.title: Iran Resisted a Powerful Attacker. Taiwan Can, Too.
item.description: By holding off America’s more powerful military, Iran showed how Taiwan can deter or defeat China.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Byman and Seth G. Jones

item.title: Lebanon Cease-Fire Leaves Netanyahu in an Uncomfortable Spot
item.description: Most Israelis wanted the fight against Hezbollah to continue. But Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s critics say he is showing that he cannot, or will not, stand up to President Trump.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Thousands of families made their way along the highway south of Beirut and Saida, Lebanon, on Friday after the cease-fire with Israel was announced.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Euan Ward and Johnatan Reiss

item.title: Trump says he might go to Pakistan if an Iran deal is signed there.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Chris Cameron
item.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump speaking to reporters outside the White House on Thursday.

item.title: 10-Day Cease-Fire Between Israel and Lebanon Goes Into Effect
item.description: The pause in the fighting between Israeli forces and Iranian-allied Hezbollah militants may help smooth the way to a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Isabel Kershner, Eric Schmitt and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Beirut, Lebanon, on Thursday. Hundreds of thousands of Lebanese have been forced by the fighting to leave their homes.

item.title: The cease-fire goes into effect after Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire.
item.description: Israeli and Lebanese officials had confirmed they would implement the truce. Hezbollah acknowledged the cease-fire, but did not directly address whether it would follow it.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar, Euan Ward, Tyler Pager and Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sánchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A man observing the aftermath of an attack in Corniche Mazraa from his home in Beirut on Monday.

item.title: Forced into a corner by the U.S., Netanyahu agrees to a cease-fire in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss

item.title: Despite Cease-Fire, Iran’s Hackers Haven’t Logged Off
item.description: Tehran’s digital warriors have continued to seek ways to gain an advantage in the conflict in a new phase of cyberspace operations.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Dustin Volz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Demonstrators in Tehran this week. Iran is trying to keep up pressure on the United States and Israel with cyberattacks but also positioning itself to mount a bigger retaliation if peace talks do not resume.

item.title: Bahrain Charges Intelligence Officer in the Death of a Detainee
item.description: The officer was charged with assault leading to death, in the case of a man who was accused by Bahraini authorities of espionage during a domestic crackdown tied to the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke rising over Bahrain’s capital, Manama, after a reported Iranian drone strike last month.

item.title: U.S. Completes Handover of Military Bases in Syria
item.description: The transition signals the end of a formal U.S. military presence in Syria for the first time in more than a decade.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:description: U.S. forces patrol near the town of Tel Hamees in Syria’s northeastern Hasakah province in 2023. The U.S. military first came to Syria in 2015 to fight the terror group Islamic State.

item.title: How Trump’s Blockade of the Strait of Hormuz Works
item.description: Our business reporter Peter Eavis breaks down how American military ships have blocked Iranian-linked vessels from using the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. encourages other vessels to make the passage.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis, Leila Medina, Nikolay Nikolov, Rafaela Balster, Stephanie Swart, Sutton Raphael, Josh Holder, Adina Renner and Blacki Migliozzi

item.title: Hegseth Again Threatens Attacks on Iran’s Civilian Infrastructure
item.description: The U.S. blockade of shipments through the Strait of Hormuz would last “for as long as it takes,” the defense secretary said.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: “We’d rather not have to do it, but we’re ready to go at the command of our president and at the push of a button,” Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Thursday.

item.title: White House Declines to Offer Congress an Estimate of Iran War Cost
item.description: Russell T. Vought, the White House budget director, said the fluctuating nature of the U.S.-Israeli war made it difficult to pinpoint expenses.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Russell T. Vought, the White House budget director, said he would not give an estimate of the cost of the conflict because he did not want to be inaccurate.

item.title: A Timeline of the Lebanon-Israel Relationship
item.description: Israeli and Lebanese officials this week met in Washington for direct talks between two nations, which have technically been at war since 1948.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Workers removing scrap metal in Beirut last week from the rubble left by Israeli bombardment.

item.title: House Again Thwarts Challenge to Trump’s Authority to Continue Iran War
item.description: The G.O.P. narrowly blocked a Democratic war powers resolution, but a senior Republican suggested that backing for the conflict is not open-ended and could wane as a statutory deadline approaches in weeks.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Megan Mineiro
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: “We are standing at the edge of a cliff, and Congress must act before this president pushes us off,” Representative Gregory W. Meeks of New York, the top Democrat on the House Foreign Affairs Committee, said ahead of the vote.

item.title: White House Shrugs Off Shaky Economy as War Exceeds Trump’s Timeline
item.description: Stocks may be soaring again, but the war in Iran has started to pinch the finances of many Americans.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm and Colby Smith
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The energy surge has made groceries more expensive while also raising costs for farmers.

item.title: Marco Rubio and Lebanese President Discuss Cease-Fire Efforts
item.description: President Trump had said that Israeli and Lebanese leaders would speak directly, but a Lebanese official said the country’s president had rejected the idea.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Francesca Regalado
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Destruction at the site of an Israeli airstrike in Beirut, Lebanon, on Tuesday.

item.title: European Airlines Face Fuel Shortages Within Weeks
item.description: If tankers do not soon begin crossing the Strait of Hormuz, airlines in Europe may not have enough jet fuel to operate all of their flights.
item.dc:creator: Niraj Chokshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ryanair, the big European low-cost airline, said that if the Strait of Hormuz remained closed into May or June, “then we cannot rule out risks to fuel supplies at some airports in Europe.”

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Tyler Pager, Johnatan Reiss and Pranav Baskar

item.title: Iran Will Be in U.S. for World Cup, FIFA’s Infantino Says
item.description: The comments come as peace talks between Iran and the United States have not yet resumed and a cease-fire is set to expire next week.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.media:description: Iran tried unsuccessfully to move its matches from the United States to Mexico.

item.title: Senate Blocks Bid to Cancel Arms Sales to Israel as Iran War Deepens Democratic Divide
item.description: Concerns over the Iran war led several Democratic senators who had rejected past bids to curb weapons transfers to Israel to vote to block the sale of bulldozers and bombs.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Bernie Sanders, independent of Vermont, has long been critical of robust American aid for Israel’s military.

item.title: Trump’s Portrayal of the War in Iran Collides With Reality
item.description: President Trump is confronting a crisis that is not bending to his narrative of a “pretty reasonable” new regime in Iran and all-but-assured victory for the United States.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump in Miami last week. Mr. Trump has declared a “regime change” accomplishment in Iran, even though analysts believe the war may have only increased the internal sway of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps.

item.title: Iran could struggle to enforce all of its latest shipping threats, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:description: A vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday. Traffic is still at a fraction of its usual levels.

item.title: Pakistani Mediators Arrive in Tehran in Bid to Keep U.S.-Iran Peace Talks Alive
item.description: The diplomacy came as the U.S. Navy locked down trade to Iranian ports, and Iran responded by threatening critical shipping routes across the region.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Eric Schmitt, Jenny Gross and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A vessel at the Strait of Hormuz, off the coast of Oman’s Musandam province, on Sunday.

item.title: Saudi Fund to Back Away From LIV Golf Under Mounting Financial Pressures
item.description: The Saudi league, established in 2022, attracted some of the sport’s biggest stars with huge contracts.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch, Vivian Nereim and Alan Blinder
item.media:description: Yasir al-Rumayyan, left, the head of Saudi Arabia’s sovereign wealth fund, presenting a trophy at the LIV Golf championship in Michigan in 2025.

item.title: With Iran War on Pause, Political Battle Resumes Over Israeli Democracy
item.description: A court fight over Itamar Ben-Gvir, an extremist who oversees the police, pits the attorney general against Benjamin Netanyahu and his right-wing government.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.media:description: Israeli Supreme Court justices on Wednesday before hearing a petition to force Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to oust the national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir.

item.title: Senate Republicans Again Block Bid to Limit Trump’s Iran War Powers
item.description: For the fourth time since the war began, G.O.P. senators successfully fended off an effort to constrain the president. But there were signs of growing unease among Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator John Fetterman of Pennsylvania was the lone Democrat to oppose taking up the measure.

item.title: Israel Considering Cease-Fire With Lebanon, Officials Say
item.description: Talk of a truce that would pause the war between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon followed rare direct talks between Israel and Lebanon in Washington on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ronen Bergman, Aaron Boxerman and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Residents collecting scrap metal from a power station destroyed by Israeli airstrikes in Tyre, Lebanon, earlier this month. While Israel recently scaled back attacks on Beirut, the Lebanese capital, it has continued striking the southern part of the country.
